Title: Static Magnetic Field Promotes Wheat Nitrogen Assimilation by Repressing Jasmonates Biosynthesis Through TaHY5

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaHDA9 physically interacts with TaHY5. 2. TaHDA9 is required as a co-repressor for TaHY5-mediated suppression of jasmonate biosynthesis gene promoters TaLOX2, TaOPR3 and TaJAR1. 3. SMF reduced H3K9ac abundance in WT but not tahy5 mutant, supporting TaHY5–TaHDA9-dependent epigenetic repression of JA biosynthesis under SMF.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts with TaHY5 to repress jasmonate biosynthesis genes, thereby contributing to reduced JA accumulation and enhanced nitrogen assimilation under SMF.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling and growth inhibition when elevated
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; split-LUC; dual-luciferase effector-reporter assay; western blot for H3K9ac; H3K9ac ChIP-seq
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Represses target genes via histone deacetylation; SMF decreases H3K9ac in a TaHY5-dependent manner, consistent with TaHDA9-mediated deacetylation at JA-pathway loci.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 16 mT static magnetic field; light versus dark
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential co-target with TaHY5 for magnetic-field-responsive nitrogen use efficiency engineering, but broad chromatin effects warrant caution.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad chromatin regulation may affect non-target processes; possible defense-growth tradeoff due to JA repression.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct genetic perturbation of TaHDA9 was not reported in this study; function inferred from interaction and promoter repression assays plus epigenetic data.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Chloroplast oxylipin / early JA biosynthesis branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxygenation of α-linolenic acid to initiate the oxylipin pathway leading to OPDA and jasmonates.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for target product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaLOX2 transcript abundance is reduced by SMF. 2. TaHY5 directly binds the TaLOX2 promoter G-box motifs. 3. TaHY5 together with TaHDA9 represses TaLOX2 promoter activity, linking SMF to lower JA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulation of TaLOX2 under SMF contributes to reduced jasmonate accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling and modulation of growth/nitrogen assimilation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated under 16 mT SMF, especially in light
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; Y1H; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 16 mT static magnetic field; light dependence
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced JA biosynthesis may weaken defense-related responses.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A useful downstream marker/target for reducing JA to improve nitrogen assimilation, but defense costs should be considered.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains G-box motifs; repression is mediated by TaHY5 with TaHDA9 rather than by TaHY5 alone.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Reduced OPDA intermediate leading to jasmonic acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces OPDA in the peroxisomal jasmonate pathway en route to jasmonic acid formation.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaOPR3 expression is significantly downregulated by SMF. 2. TaHY5 directly binds TaOPR3 promoter G-box motifs. 3. TaHY5 and TaHDA9 co-repress TaOPR3 promoter activity, contributing to lower JA levels under SMF.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced TaOPR3 expression under SMF contributes to repression of JA biosynthesis and improved nitrogen assimilation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated under 16 mT SMF, especially in light
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; Y1H; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 16 mT static magnetic field; light dependence
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower JA may compromise defense functions.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream engineering node for JA reduction to support nitrogen use efficiency under controlled conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains G-box elements directly recognized by TaHY5.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH3 acyl acid amido synthetase family
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Conjugates jasmonic acid to isoleucine to form bioactive JA-Ile.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaJAR1 expression is significantly reduced by SMF. 2. TaHY5 directly binds G-box motifs in the TaJAR1 promoter by EMSA. 3. TaHY5 plus TaHDA9 represses TaJAR1 promoter activity, linking SMF to reduced JA-Ile production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulation of TaJAR1 under SMF reduces active JA-Ile and supports enhanced nitrogen assimilation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive jasmonate signal promoting JA responses
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated under 16 mT SMF, especially in light
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 16 mT static magnetic field; light dependence
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced JA-Ile signaling may impair defense-related outputs.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for modulating JA activity to improve nutrient assimilation, with caution regarding defense tradeoffs.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Y1H could not be performed due to promoter self-activation, but direct binding was supported by EMSA.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported by enzyme activity measurements
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces nitrate to nitrite during primary nitrogen assimilation.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for ammonium assimilation and nitrogen incorporation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaNR is upregulated by SMF in roots and its enzyme activity increases under SMF. 2. Exogenous JA blocks the SMF-induced increase in TaNR expression/activity. 3. TaHY5 directly activates the TaNR promoter, and TaHY5 loss abolishes SMF responsiveness of TaNR.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes nitrate assimilation under SMF, contributing to higher nitrogen content and growth.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Nitrogen assimilation products / ammonium-derived biomass nitrogen
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports growth, chlorophyll accumulation, and nitrogen use efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by SMF, especially in roots and in light
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; NR activity assay; JA treatment assay; CRISPR knockout analysis; overexpression analysis; dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 16 mT static magnetic field; low nitrogen; light versus dark; exogenous JA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for improving nitrogen use efficiency, especially combined with TaHY5-mediated regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains G-box motifs and is directly activated by TaHY5.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces nitrite to ammonium for nitrogen assimilation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaNiR transcript levels are strongly induced by SMF. 2. Exogenous JA suppresses SMF-mediated induction of TaNiR. 3. TaHY5 directly activates the TaNiR promoter, and knockout of TaHY5 attenuates/eliminates this response.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances reduced nitrogen production under SMF, contributing to increased nitrogen assimilation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports amino acid and biomass synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by SMF, especially during light
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; JA treatment assay; CRISPR knockout analysis; overexpression analysis; dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 16 mT static magnetic field; low nitrogen; light versus dark; exogenous JA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Good target for boosting nitrogen assimilation in combination with TaHY5-based strategies.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains G-box motifs directly activated by TaHY5.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Repression of jasmonate biosynthesis and activation of nitrate assimilation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses TaLOX2, TaOPR3 and TaJAR1 via recruitment of TaHDA9; activates TaNR and TaNiR; does not directly regulate TaNRT2.5
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression with chromatin deacetylation cofactor for JA genes; transcriptional activation for nitrogen assimilation genes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms functional regulatory module with TaHDA9
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Core light-signaling regulator required for SMF-induced reduction of jasmonates and promotion of nitrogen assimilation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaHY5 is the most responsive light-signaling factor identified under SMF and its induction is light dependent. 2. CRISPR knockout of TaHY5 abolishes SMF-promoted growth, JA reduction, NR activity increase, nitrogen accumulation, and expression changes of key JA and N genes. 3. TaHY5 directly binds promoters of TaLOX2, TaOPR3 and TaJAR1 and, together with TaHDA9, represses these JA biosynthesis genes. 4. TaHY5 directly activates TaNR and TaNiR promoters, linking light/magnetic signaling to nitrogen assimilation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for SMF to lower JA/JA-Ile and enhance nitrogen assimilation and wheat seedling growth.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: JA restrains growth and affects defense; enhanced nitrogen assimilation supports growth and nutrient use efficiency
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by SMF primarily during the light period
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; qRT-PCR; EMSA; Y1H; Y2H; split-LUC; dual-luciferase assays; hormone/metabolite profiling; NR activity assay; nitrate flux assay; ChIP-seq; western blot
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Functions upstream of epigenetic repression by recruiting TaHDA9, causing reduced H3K9ac at JA-pathway genes under SMF.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 16 mT static magnetic field; light versus dark; exogenous JA; low nitrogen
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value regulatory target to improve nitrogen use efficiency under magnetic treatment or by molecular engineering, but balancing JA-mediated defense is important.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: HY5 knockout alters plant height, chlorophyll content and light-response genes; reducing JA may create growth-defense tradeoffs.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TaHY5 alone showed transcriptional activation capacity in yeast and did not repress JA gene reporters alone, indicating repression requires cofactor TaHDA9.
